3.happening: Create an empty list of next-pointers.
3.why: Each node needs to know which node follows it — that is what makes it a linked list rather than an array.
3.invariant: node_links[k] will hold the index of the node after k.

4.why: We wire the chain up one node at a time.
4.invariant: i is the node whose link is being built.

5.why: Every node needs a next-pointer before we can walk the chain.

5.happening: Every node is wired — the chain is complete.
5.why: The list can now be traversed by following next-pointers.
5.invariant: node_links has one entry per node.

6.why: In a singly linked list each node knows only its immediate successor.
6.invariant: Links always point forward, never backward.

7.invariant: Each node is wired exactly once.

8.happening: The last node ({len(head) - 1}) points at nothing — set its link to -1.
8.why: -1 is our stand-in for null; it is the signal that tells fast when it has run out of list.
8.invariant: Exactly one node has a null next-pointer: the tail.

9.happening: Place the SLOW cursor on the head node.
9.why: Both cursors start from the same place — the gap between them is created purely by their different speeds.
9.invariant: slow always sits on a real node.

10.happening: Place the FAST cursor on the head node too.
10.why: They start together, so after k rounds slow has moved k nodes and fast has moved 2k.
10.invariant: fast is always at least as far along as slow.

11.happening: Declare where slow last hopped from.
11.why: The visual draws slow's single hop as an arc from this node to its new one.
11.invariant: slow_from is slow's position at the start of the round.

12.happening: Declare where fast last hopped from.
12.why: The visual draws fast's DOUBLE hop as a two-humped arc starting here.
12.invariant: fast_from is fast's position at the start of the round.

13.why: After {hops} rounds slow has travelled {hops} nodes and fast has travelled twice that.
13.invariant: fast's distance is always exactly double slow's.

14.why: It is filled once fast runs out of list.
14.invariant: result is only meaningful after the loop ends.

15.happening: Fast is on node {fast} and it has a next node — there is room for another double hop.
15.why: Fast needs TWO nodes ahead of it to move twice; checking both conditions is what keeps it from running off the end.
15.invariant: slow has travelled exactly half as far as fast.

15.happening: Fast has run out of list — stop.
15.why: Fast covered the whole list while slow covered half, so slow is standing on the middle.
15.invariant: slow is now the middle node.

16.happening: Remember that fast is standing on node {fast}.
16.why: So its two-hop journey can be drawn as one continuous double arc rather than a teleport.
16.invariant: fast_from is where this round's double hop begins.

17.why: This is only the first half of fast's move — it is not done yet.
17.invariant: fast is mid-move; the round is not complete.

18.happening: FAST hops AGAIN: node {fast} → node {node_links[fast]}. That is two nodes in one round.
18.why: Two hops to slow's one is the entire trick — when fast reaches the end, slow can only have reached the middle.
18.invariant: fast advances exactly 2 nodes per round.

19.happening: Remember that slow is standing on node {slow}.
19.why: So its single hop can be drawn as a move from here to there.
19.invariant: slow_from is where this round's single hop begins.

20.why: One hop per round. Fast has just moved two, so the gap between them widens by exactly one node.
20.invariant: slow advances exactly 1 node per round — always half of fast.

21.happening: Round {hops + 1} complete — slow has moved {hops + 1}, fast has moved {(hops + 1) * 2}.
21.why: The gap between them widens by exactly one node every round.
21.invariant: fast's distance is always double slow's.

22.happening: Fast is spent and slow stopped on node {slow} — take the list from there onward.
22.why: Returning a node in a linked list means returning that node and everything reachable from it.
22.invariant: slow travelled exactly half the list, so it is the middle.

23.happening: The middle node is {head[slow]}; the answer is {result}.
23.why: One pass, two cursors, no counting and no second traversal.
23.invariant: For an even-length list this lands on the SECOND of the two middles, exactly as required.

init.why: The cursors need real links to follow.

loop.happening: Checking whether fast still has two nodes ahead of it.
loop.why: Fast stops the moment it cannot complete another double hop.
loop.invariant: slow has travelled exactly half as far as fast.

update.happening: Recording a cursor's starting position or completing a round.
update.why: The visual needs to know where each hop began to draw the motion.
update.invariant: Both cursors only ever move forward.

move.happening: Advancing a cursor — slow by one node, fast by two.
move.why: The difference in speed is what locates the middle in a single pass.
move.invariant: fast advances exactly twice as far as slow each round.

return.happening: Returning the middle node and everything after it.
return.why: Fast reached the end, so slow reached the halfway point.
return.invariant: The answer starts at the middle node.
